fields.description:
## Context / Problem

The deterministic Pass 3 of code-review ignores project routing. `code_review_decide` passes
`registry.threshold_for` as a bare callable and calls `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria()` with no
arguments (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:419,427`), so both read the **packaged**
routing map and a project overlay's threshold / blocking / nit-suppression re-tunes are dropped —
even though those resolvers already accept an optional routing map
(`src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py:309-343`) and `registry.effective_routing(repo_root)`
already exists on `origin/main` (`registry.py:171-176`). Third of five seams (root cause on
superseded bug 8a4b).

## Approach

Pure wiring — thread the repository-effective routing map into the two Pass-3 resolvers:

- In `code_review_decide`, compute `routing = registry.effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)`
  (`ctx.repo_root` is already used in this function at `workflow_ops.py:414`).
- Bind that map into the `threshold_for` callable handed to `pass3_over_findings` (e.g.
  `functools.partial(registry.threshold_for, routing_map=routing)`), and pass it to
  `registry.nit_suppressed_criteria(routing)`.

No `registry.py` change is needed — the optional `routing_map` parameters landed with story 5065,
and `effective_routing` is on `origin/main` today (cited above).

**Rejected alternative.** Re-implementing routing resolution inside `workflow_ops` — the shared
resolvers already exist and are the single source of truth; duplicating them would diverge from
plan-review's routing semantics.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` — thread `effective_routing(ctx.repo_root)` into the
  Pass-3 `threshold_for` binding and `nit_suppressed_criteria` call.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — extend this shared test file (a new file
  first created earlier in the stack; this ticket appends its Pass-3 routing cases). This ticket
  **depends on** child 2 `[rebar:761e-5af2-ed57-408a]`, which will introduce both the synthetic-repo
  fixture and the logical `project.<name>` attribution on code-review findings; the Pass-3 tests
  here consume a finding carrying that logical id.

## Testing

- Happy path (routing): a synthetic repo re-tunes `project.foo` (lower block threshold + blocking,
  and separately `nit_suppressed`); assert Pass 3 blocks / demotes the project finding per the
  **project** routing, not the packaged default. The routed finding carries the logical
  `project.foo` id that child 2 `[rebar:761e-5af2-ed57-408a]` will attribute.
- Parity: with no overlay, Pass-3 dispositions are byte-identical to today (packaged-default path).

Tier: unit; feeds deterministic findings (carrying logical `project.foo`) through
`code_review_decide` with a synthetic effective routing — no LLM.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Project routing (threshold / blocking / nit-suppression) governs the deterministic Pass 3
      for a project finding; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k routing`.
- [ ] With no overlay, Pass-3 dispositions are unchanged (parity); verified by the same `-k routing`
      selector asserting the packaged-default path.
